data.title: Founder of cloud mining service HashFlare pleads guilty to $577 million cryptocurrency Ponzi scheme, faces 20 years in prison
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The U.S. Department of Justice announced that the operators of the defunct cloud mining service HashFlare, Estonian citizens Sergei Potapenko and Ivan Turõgin, admitted to participating in a multi-level cryptocurrency Ponzi scheme involving $577 million. HashFlare provided cloud mining services between 2015 and 2019, but its actual mining capacity was far lower than advertised, and its Bitcoin mining computing power was less than 1% of what it claimed. The company also defrauded investments through the fake crypto bank Polybius and failed to pay dividends to customers, causing losses to hundreds of thousands of victims worldwide. The two were arrested in Estonia in November 2022 and extradited to the United States. They pleaded guilty to conspiracy to commit wire fraud and will face up to 20 years in prison and confiscate assets worth more than $400 million to compensate victims. (The Block)

data.title: RedStone Token (RED) is now available on Gate.io’s pre-market OTC trading
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, Gate.io pre-market OTC trading has officially launched RedStone Token (RED) at 17:00 (UTC+8) on February 14, 2025, providing users with a convenient channel to participate in the RedStone ecosystem. It is reported that RedStone is the fastest growing oracle and a major participant in providing active verification services and restaking. In addition, Restone is also a leader in the oracle field of yield assets, covering LRTs, LSTs, and stablecoins such as Ethena and Elixir.

data.title: Short-Term Bitcoin Holders Accumulate Over 4 Million BTC
data.description:
According to CoinDesk, short-term bitcoin (BTC) holders have significantly increased their holdings, adding 1.5 million BTC since September. This brings the total to over 4 million BTC, with an average monthly accumulation of approximately 300,000 BTC. During this period, bitcoin's price experienced a notable surge from $60,000 to $109,000 before retreating below $100,000. Glassnode defines short-term holders as those who have held bitcoin for less than 155 days.

Historically, bitcoin's price tends to reach its peak when short-term holders exhaust their buying momentum, leading to a slowdown in price appreciation. This pattern has been observed in previous bull market cycles, notably in 2013, 2017, and 2021. During these cycles, short-term holders held significantly more bitcoin at the peaks: 5 million BTC in 2013, 6.2 million BTC in 2017, and 4.6 million BTC in 2021. In comparison, the current holdings of 4 million BTC are relatively low, suggesting that new market entrants may continue to accumulate, indicating potential for further growth in the cycle.

Meanwhile, long-term holders, defined as investors who have held bitcoin for more than 155 days, have distributed 1.2 million BTC during the same period. This distribution reflects significant profit-taking following bitcoin's strong rally since November. The ongoing offloading by long-term holders has been a major factor contributing to bitcoin's stalled price action since mid-November.

data.title: Altcoins Surge Amid ETF Optimism And Buyback Initiatives
data.description:
According to CoinDesk, alternative cryptocurrencies, notably XRP and Dogecoin (DOGE), experienced significant gains on Friday, driven by optimism surrounding spot ETF applications and strategic buyback programs. The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has acknowledged Grayscale's applications for XRP and DOGE spot ETFs, as indicated by updates from the market regulator on February 13. These applications will now be submitted to the SEC's federal register, initiating a 240-day period for review and decision-making.

The recent surge in altcoin ETF filings, including those for Solana's SOL and Litecoin (LTC), reflects growing expectations for the integration of cryptocurrencies into mainstream finance under U.S. President Donald Trump's administration. The potential approval of XRP and DOGE spot ETFs, which are regulated and familiar investment vehicles, could simplify institutional exposure to these cryptocurrencies without the need for direct purchase and storage. This development is anticipated to enhance market liquidity and increase demand for these tokens.

XRP saw a notable increase, trading at $2.73, marking a 10% rise over a 24-hour period, making it the best-performing cryptocurrency among the top 100 by market value. DOGE also experienced a 4% increase, as reported by CoinDesk and Coingecko. Analyst Valentin Fournier from BRN highlighted the SEC's acceptance of ETF applications for XRP and Dogecoin as a significant development, adding to the growing list of altcoin ETF reviews, including Solana and Litecoin. If approved, these products could significantly expand institutional access to altcoins, potentially setting the stage for an alt-season later this year.

In addition to regulatory developments, Jupiter's JUP token also saw a 10% increase, driven by the decentralized exchange's plan to allocate 50% of its protocol fees toward repurchasing and locking JUP tokens for three years starting February 17. This initiative aims to reduce the token's circulating supply and enhance the platform's sustainability.

Meanwhile, Bitcoin (BTC) continued to trade around $97,000, showing lackluster performance amid ongoing outflows from U.S.-listed spot exchange-traded funds (ETFs). The 11 spot BTC ETFs listed in the U.S. have experienced a cumulative net outflow of $650 million this week, according to Farisde Investors. Despite the subdued price action, there is optimism against the backdrop of higher-than-expected U.S. CPI and PPI releases this week. Fournier expressed a bullish outlook, citing Bitcoin's resilience in the face of high inflation and improving regulatory clarity, suggesting that this accumulation phase may lead to a strong rally in the coming weeks.

data.title: Bitget has launched U-based HEI, WAVES, and BURGER perpetual contracts
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to the official announcement, Bitget announced that it has launched U-based HEI, WAVES, and BURGER perpetual contracts. Contract strategy trading will be opened simultaneously.

data.title: CZ has deleted the long article posted 20 minutes ago
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News X platform shows that CZ has deleted the long article published 20 minutes ago. The key content of the previous long article includes: 
1. People say they like meme. What they mean is that they like you to tell them which token to buy to make money quickly. ("Which CA?" question) Unfortunately, I can't do it, I won't do it, and I don't know how to do it.
2. Welcome to the decentralized world. But once again, I hope that the ecosystem can develop naturally.
3. As of the time of writing, according to Dex Screener, there is a Broccoli project on the BNB chain with a market value of more than $200 million. Not sure if this is a good thing or a bad thing.
4. Please manage your risks, if you can't afford the downward volatility, don't touch the meme coin.

data.title: CZ: I can’t tweet anymore, people always over-interpret my tweets, and anything I mention will have extra meaning attached to it
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News CZ posted on X platform: "I can't tweet anymore. People always over-interpret my tweets. Anything I mention will be given some extra meaning."

data.description:

data.title: Bitget will provide exclusive subsidies for FTX creditor users, with a maximum rebate of $2,500
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Bitget launches an exclusive subsidy event for FTX creditor users. During the event, users only need to submit creditor information and use the creditor's email to complete the registration. During the review period, users can recharge to the latest registered Bitget account without waiting. Bitget will automatically calculate the net recharge amount of eligible users during the event (recharge amount during the event - withdrawal amount during the event) and issue rewards according to the ladder, with a maximum subsidy of US$2,500. The event time is from 00:00 on February 18 to 00:00 on March 5 (UTC+8).

data.title: OKX adjusts the minimum price accuracy of some currency pairs
data.description: According to the official announcement, OKX will adjust the minimum price accuracy of some currency pairs, such as currency pairs, leveraged pairs, and perpetual contract trading pairs from 2:00 to 4:00 pm (UTC+8) on February 18, 2025. Specific adjustments include the minimum price accuracy adjustment of currency pairs such as USDT/TRY, and some currency pairs will suspend trading for 2 minutes. (This newsletter is generated by AI)
